briefdescription.para: USB device support middle layer runtime state.

detaileddescription.para: Part of USB device states without suspended and powered states, as it is better to track them separately.

briefdescription.para: Device supports or is connected to a full speed bus.
Device supports or is connected to a high speed bus
Device supports or is connected to a super speed bus.
USB device speed.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* usbd_msg_cb_t) (struct usbd_context *const ctx, const struct usbd_msg *const msg)
memberdef.argsstring: )(struct usbd_context *const ctx, const struct usbd_msg *const msg)
memberdef.name: usbd_msg_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Callback type definition for USB device message delivery.

detaileddescription.para: If the Kconfig option USBD_MSG_DEFERRED_MODE is enabled, then the callback is executed in the context of the system workqueue. Notification messages are stored in a queue and delivered to the callback in sequence.

If the Kconfig option USBD_MSG_DEFERRED_MODE is disabled, the callback is executed in the context of the USB device stack thread. The user should make sure that the callback execution does not block or disrupt device stack handling.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

briefdescription.para: Get the USB device runtime context under which the class is registered.

detaileddescription.para: The class implementation must use this function and not access the members of the struct directly.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device class data

simplesect.para: Pointer to USB device runtime context

briefdescription.para: Get class implementation private data.

detaileddescription.para: The class implementation must use this function and not access the members of the struct directly.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device class data

simplesect.para: Pointer to class implementation private data

briefdescription.para: Add common USB descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: Add common descriptor like string or BOS Device Capability.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

briefdescription.para: Get USB string descriptor index from descriptor node.

simplesect.para: Descriptor index, 0 if descriptor is not part of any device

briefdescription.para: Remove USB string descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: Remove linked USB string descriptor from any list.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct usbd_context *uds_ctx, const enum usbd_speed speed, struct usbd_config_node *cd)
memberdef.name: usbd_add_configuration

briefdescription.para: Add a USB device configuration.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context
Speed at which this configuration operates

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct usbd_context *uds_ctx, const char *name, const enum usbd_speed speed, uint8_t cfg)
memberdef.name: usbd_register_class

briefdescription.para: Register an USB class instance.

detaileddescription.para: An USB class implementation can have one or more instances. To identify the instances we use device drivers API. Device names have a prefix derived from the name of the class, for example CDC_ACM for CDC ACM class instance, and can also be easily identified in the shell. Class instance can only be registered when the USB device stack is disabled. Registered instances are initialized at initialization of the USB device stack, and the interface descriptors of each instance are adapted to the whole context.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct usbd_context *uds_ctx, const enum usbd_speed speed, uint8_t cfg, const char *const blocklist[])
memberdef.name: usbd_register_all_classes

briefdescription.para: Register all available USB class instances.

detaileddescription.para: Register all available instances. Like usbd_register_class, but does not take the instance name and instead registers all available instances.

This cannot be combined. If your application calls usbd_register_class for any device, configuration number, or instance, either usbd_register_class or this function will fail.
There may be situations where a particular function should not be registered, for example, when using the USB DFU implementation, the DFU mode function must be excluded during normal device operation. To do this, the device can pass a blocklist in the form shown below as an optional argument. If the blocklist is not needed, the argument should be NULL.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context
Null pointer terminated array of pointers to string literals to be used as a block list

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct usbd_context *uds_ctx, const char *name, const enum usbd_speed speed, uint8_t cfg)
memberdef.name: usbd_unregister_class

briefdescription.para: Unregister an USB class instance.

detaileddescription.para: USB class instance will be removed and will not appear on the next start of the stack. Instance can only be unregistered when the USB device stack is disabled.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct usbd_context *uds_ctx, const enum usbd_speed speed, uint8_t cfg)
memberdef.name: usbd_unregister_all_classes

briefdescription.para: Unregister all available USB class instances.

detaileddescription.para: Unregister all available instances. Like usbd_unregister_class, but does not take the instance name and instead unregisters all available instances.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

briefdescription.para: Register USB notification message callback.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

briefdescription.para: Initialize USB device.

detaileddescription.para: Initialize USB device descriptors and configuration, initialize USB device controller. Class instances should be registered before they are involved. However, the stack should also initialize without registered instances, even if the host would complain about missing interfaces.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

briefdescription.para: Enable the USB device support and registered class instances.

detaileddescription.para: This function enables the USB device support.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

briefdescription.para: Disable the USB device support.

detaileddescription.para: This function disables the USB device support.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

briefdescription.para: Shutdown the USB device support.

detaileddescription.para: This function completely disables the USB device support.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

briefdescription.para: Halt endpoint.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or error from udc_ep_set_halt()

briefdescription.para: Clear endpoint halt.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or error from udc_ep_clear_halt()

briefdescription.para: Checks whether the endpoint is halted.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: true if endpoint is halted, false otherwise

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct usbd_class_data *const c_data, const uint8_t ep, const size_t size)
memberdef.name: usbd_ep_buf_alloc

briefdescription.para: Allocate buffer for USB device request.

detaileddescription.para: Allocate a new buffer from controller's driver buffer pool.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device class data

simplesect.para: pointer to allocated request or NULL on error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct usbd_context *const uds_ctx, const size_t size)
memberdef.name: usbd_ep_ctrl_data_in_alloc

briefdescription.para: Allocate buffer for USB control transfer data stage.

detaileddescription.para: Allocate a new buffer from controller's driver buffer pool.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: pointer to allocated request or NULL on error.

briefdescription.para: Queue USB device control request.

detaileddescription.para: Add control request to the queue.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, all other values should be treated as error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct usbd_class_data *const c_data, struct net_buf *const buf)
memberdef.name: usbd_ep_enqueue

briefdescription.para: Queue USB device request.

detaileddescription.para: Add request to the queue.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device class data

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or error from udc_ep_enqueue()

briefdescription.para: Remove all USB device controller requests from endpoint queue.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or error from udc_ep_dequeue()

briefdescription.para: Free USB device request buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Put the buffer back into the request buffer pool.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, all other values should be treated as error.

briefdescription.para: Checks whether the USB device controller is suspended.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: true if endpoint is halted, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Initiate the USB remote wakeup (TBD)

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

briefdescription.para: Set the self-powered status of the USB device.

detaileddescription.para: The status is used in the Self Powered field of the Get Status request response to indicate whether the device is currently self-powered.

parameterdescription.para: Sets self-powered status if true, clears it otherwise

briefdescription.para: Get actual device speed.

simplesect.para: Actual device speed

briefdescription.para: Get highest speed supported by the controller.

simplesect.para: Highest supported speed

memberdef.argsstring: (struct usbd_context *const uds_ctx, const enum usbd_speed speed, const uint16_t bcd)
memberdef.name: usbd_device_set_bcd_usb

briefdescription.para: Set USB device descriptor value bcdUSB.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context
Speed for which the bcdUSB should be set

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

briefdescription.para: Set USB device descriptor value idVendor.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

briefdescription.para: Set USB device descriptor value idProduct.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

briefdescription.para: Set USB device descriptor value bcdDevice.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct usbd_context *const uds_ctx, const enum usbd_speed speed, const uint8_t base_class, const uint8_t subclass, const uint8_t protocol)
memberdef.name: usbd_device_set_code_triple

briefdescription.para: Set USB device descriptor code triple Base Class, SubClass, and Protocol.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context
Speed for which the code triple should be set

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct usbd_context *const uds_ctx, const enum usbd_speed speed, const uint8_t cfg, const bool enable)
memberdef.name: usbd_config_attrib_rwup

briefdescription.para: Setup USB device configuration attribute Remote Wakeup.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context
Sets attribute if true, clears it otherwise

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct usbd_context *const uds_ctx, const enum usbd_speed speed, const uint8_t cfg, const bool enable)
memberdef.name: usbd_config_attrib_self

briefdescription.para: Setup USB device configuration attribute Self-powered.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context
Sets attribute if true, clears it otherwise

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct usbd_context *const uds_ctx, const enum usbd_speed speed, const uint8_t cfg, const uint8_t power)
memberdef.name: usbd_config_maxpower

briefdescription.para: Setup USB device configuration power consumption.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

briefdescription.para: Check that the controller can detect the VBUS state change.

detaileddescription.para: This can be used in a generic application to explicitly handle the VBUS detected event after usbd_init(). For example, to call usbd_enable() after a short delay to give the PMIC time to detect the bus, or to handle cases where usbd_enable() can only be called after a VBUS detected event.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: true if controller can detect VBUS state change, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Register an USB vendor request with recipient device.

detaileddescription.para: The vendor request with the recipient device applies to all configurations within the device.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to USB device support context

simplesect.para: 0 on success, other values on fail.

briefdescription.para: USB Class instance registered flag.

memberdef.name: USBD_DEVICE_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: static struct usb_device_descriptor \ fs_desc_##device_name = { \ .bLength = sizeof(struct usb_device_descriptor), \ .bDescriptorType = USB_DESC_DEVICE, \ .bcdUSB = sys_cpu_to_le16(USB_SRN_2_0), \ .bDeviceClass = USB_BCC_MISCELLANEOUS, \ .bDeviceSubClass = 2, \ .bDeviceProtocol = 1, \ .bMaxPacketSize0 = USB_CONTROL_EP_MPS, \ .idVendor = vid, \ .idProduct = pid, \ .bcdDevice = sys_cpu_to_le16(USB_BCD_DRN), \ .iManufacturer = 0, \ .iProduct = 0, \ .iSerialNumber = 0, \ .bNumConfigurations = 0, \ }; \ IF_ENABLED(USBD_SUPPORTS_HIGH_SPEED, ( \ static struct usb_device_descriptor \ hs_desc_##device_name = { \ .bLength = sizeof(struct usb_device_descriptor), \ .bDescriptorType = USB_DESC_DEVICE, \ .bcdUSB = sys_cpu_to_le16(USB_SRN_2_0), \ .bDeviceClass = USB_BCC_MISCELLANEOUS, \ .bDeviceSubClass = 2, \ .bDeviceProtocol = 1, \ .bMaxPacketSize0 = 64, \ .idVendor = vid, \ .idProduct = pid, \ .bcdDevice = sys_cpu_to_le16(USB_BCD_DRN), \ .iManufacturer = 0, \ .iProduct = 0, \ .iSerialNumber = 0, \ .bNumConfigurations = 0, \ }; \ )) \ static STRUCT_SECTION_ITERABLE(usbd_context, device_name) = { \ .name = STRINGIFY(device_name), \ .dev = udc_dev, \ .fs_desc = &fs_desc_##device_name, \ IF_ENABLED(USBD_SUPPORTS_HIGH_SPEED, ( \ .hs_desc = &hs_desc_##device_name, \ )) \ }

briefdescription.para: Define USB device context structure.

detaileddescription.para: Macro defines a USB device structure needed by the stack to manage its properties and runtime data. The vid and pid parameters can also be changed using usbd_device_set_vid() and usbd_device_set_pid().
Example of use:

memberdef.name: USBD_CONFIGURATION_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: static struct usb_cfg_descriptor \ cfg_desc_##name = { \ .bLength = sizeof(struct usb_cfg_descriptor), \ .bDescriptorType = USB_DESC_CONFIGURATION, \ .wTotalLength = 0, \ .bNumInterfaces = 0, \ .bConfigurationValue = 1, \ .iConfiguration = 0, \ .bmAttributes = USB_SCD_RESERVED | (attrib), \ .bMaxPower = (power), \ }; \ BUILD_ASSERT((power) < 256, "Too much power"); \ static struct usbd_config_node name = { \ .desc = &cfg_desc_##name, \ .str_desc_nd = desc_nd, \ }

briefdescription.para: Define USB device configuration.

detaileddescription.para: USB device requires at least one configuration instance per supported speed. attrib is a combination of USB_SCD_SELF_POWERED or USB_SCD_REMOTE_WAKEUP, depending on which characteristic the USB device should have in this configuration.

parameterdescription.para: Configuration characteristics. Attributes can also be updated with usbd_config_attrib_rwup() and usbd_config_attrib_self()
bMaxPower value in 2 mA units. This value can also be set with usbd_config_maxpower()
Address of the string descriptor node used to describe the configuration, see USBD_DESC_CONFIG_DEFINE(). String descriptors are optional and the parameter can be NULL.

memberdef.name: USBD_DESC_LANG_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: static const uint16_t langid_##name = sys_cpu_to_le16(0x0409); \ static struct usbd_desc_node name = { \ .str = { \ .idx = 0, \ .utype = USBD_DUT_STRING_LANG, \ }, \ .ptr = &langid_##name, \ .bLength = sizeof(struct usb_string_descriptor), \ .bDescriptorType = USB_DESC_STRING, \ }

briefdescription.para: Create a string descriptor node and language string descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: This macro defines a descriptor node and a string descriptor that, when added to the device context, is automatically used as the language string descriptor zero. Both descriptor node and descriptor are defined with static-storage-class specifier. Default and currently only supported language ID is 0x0409 English (United States). If string descriptors are used, it is necessary to add this descriptor as the first one to the USB device context.

memberdef.name: USBD_DESC_STRING_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: static const uint8_t ascii_##d_name[sizeof(d_string)] = d_string; \ static struct usbd_desc_node d_name = { \ .str = { \ .utype = d_utype, \ .ascii7 = true, \ }, \ .ptr = &ascii_##d_name, \ .bLength = USB_STRING_DESCRIPTOR_LENGTH(d_string), \ .bDescriptorType = USB_DESC_STRING, \ }

briefdescription.para: Create a string descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: This macro defines a descriptor node and a string descriptor. The string literal passed to the macro should be in the ASCII7 format. It is converted to UTF16LE format on the host request.

parameterdescription.para: Internal string descriptor node identifier name

briefdescription.para: Create a string descriptor node and manufacturer string descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: This macro defines a descriptor node and a string descriptor that, when added to the device context, is automatically used as the manufacturer string descriptor. Both descriptor node and descriptor are defined with static-storage-class specifier.

briefdescription.para: Create a string descriptor node and product string descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: This macro defines a descriptor node and a string descriptor that, when added to the device context, is automatically used as the product string descriptor. Both descriptor node and descriptor are defined with static-storage-class specifier.

memberdef.name: USBD_DESC_SERIAL_NUMBER_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: BUILD_ASSERT(IS_ENABLED(CONFIG_HWINFO), "HWINFO not enabled"); \ static struct usbd_desc_node d_name = { \ .str = { \ .utype = USBD_DUT_STRING_SERIAL_NUMBER, \ .ascii7 = true, \ .use_hwinfo = true, \ }, \ .bDescriptorType = USB_DESC_STRING, \ }

briefdescription.para: Create a string descriptor node and serial number string descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: This macro defines a descriptor node that, when added to the device context, is automatically used as the serial number string descriptor. A valid serial number is obtained from Hardware Info whenever this string descriptor is requested.

simplesect.para: The HWINFO driver must be available and the Kconfig option HWINFO enabled.

briefdescription.para: Create a string descriptor node for configuration descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: This macro defines a descriptor node whose address can be used as an argument for the USBD_CONFIGURATION_DEFINE() macro.

parameterdescription.para: ASCII7 encoded configuration description string literal

memberdef.name: USBD_DESC_BOS_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: BUILD_ASSERT(IS_ENABLED(CONFIG_USBD_BOS_SUPPORT), \ "USB device BOS support is disabled"); \ static struct usbd_desc_node name = { \ .bos = { \ .utype = USBD_DUT_BOS_NONE, \ }, \ .ptr = subset, \ .bLength = len, \ .bDescriptorType = USB_DESC_BOS, \ }

briefdescription.para: Define BOS Device Capability descriptor node.

detaileddescription.para: The application defines a BOS capability descriptor node for descriptors such as USB 2.0 Extension Descriptor.

simplesect.para: It requires Kconfig options USBD_BOS_SUPPORT to be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a Device Capability descriptor

memberdef.name: USBD_VREQUEST_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: BUILD_ASSERT(IS_ENABLED(CONFIG_USBD_VREQ_SUPPORT), \ "USB device vendor request support is disabled"); \ static struct usbd_vreq_node name = { \ .code = vcode, \ .to_host = vto_host, \ .to_dev = vto_dev, \ }

briefdescription.para: Define a vendor request with recipient device.

simplesect.para: It requires Kconfig options USBD_VREQ_SUPPORT to be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: Vendor callback for to-host direction request
Vendor callback for to-device direction request

memberdef.name: USBD_DESC_BOS_VREQ_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: BUILD_ASSERT(IS_ENABLED(CONFIG_USBD_BOS_SUPPORT), \ "USB device BOS support is disabled"); \ USBD_VREQUEST_DEFINE(vreq_nd_##name, vcode, vto_host, vto_dev); \ static struct usbd_desc_node name = { \ .bos = { \ .utype = USBD_DUT_BOS_VREQ, \ .vreq_nd = &vreq_nd_##name, \ }, \ .ptr = subset, \ .bLength = len, \ .bDescriptorType = USB_DESC_BOS, \ }

briefdescription.para: Define BOS Device Capability descriptor node with vendor request.

detaileddescription.para: This macro defines a BOS descriptor, usually a platform capability, with a vendor request node.
USBD_DESC_BOS_VREQ_DEFINE(bos_vreq_webusb, sizeof(bos_cap_webusb), &bos_cap_webusb, SAMPLE_WEBUSB_VENDOR_CODE, webusb_to_host_cb, NULL);

simplesect.para: It requires Kconfig options USBD_VREQ_SUPPORT and USBD_BOS_SUPPORT to be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a Device Capability descriptor
Vendor callback for to-host direction request
Vendor callback for to-device direction request

memberdef.name: USBD_DEFINE_CLASS
memberdef.initializer: static struct usbd_class_data class_name = { \ .name = STRINGIFY(class_name), \ .api = class_api, \ .v_reqs = class_v_reqs, \ .priv = class_priv, \ }; \ static STRUCT_SECTION_ITERABLE_ALTERNATE( \ usbd_class_fs, usbd_class_node, class_name##_fs) = { \ .c_data = &class_name, \ }; \ IF_ENABLED(USBD_SUPPORTS_HIGH_SPEED, ( \ static STRUCT_SECTION_ITERABLE_ALTERNATE( \ usbd_class_hs, usbd_class_node, class_name##_hs) = { \ .c_data = &class_name, \ } \ ))

briefdescription.para: Define USB device support class data.

detaileddescription.para: Macro defines class (function) data, as well as corresponding node structures used internally by the stack.

briefdescription.para: Helper to declare request table of usbd_cctx_vendor_req.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the vendor request field

briefdescription.para: Helper to declare supported vendor requests.
New USB device stack core API.
